steps.title: Identify the problem-solution contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes that Cristall was “largely overlooked” by scholars, then pivots to a 2020 analysis that “helps illuminate” her work. This shift from problem (neglect) to solution (illumination) is the key signal guiding the transition.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Addressing this oversight” fits this perfectly—”oversight” directly refers to the problem of being overlooked, and “addressing” shows the analysis responds to and corrects it. The other three choices misrepresent this relationship, either suggesting the analysis demonstrates neglect, agrees with it, or simply supplements prior research rather than contrasting with it. That matches choice D — D is correct.
